Kendall accepted Ranch+Buffalo 4/3 and is completing forms for all 3 SKUs. Onboarding docs in progress as of 4/15; TraceGains access still blocked.

status: LIVE ACCOUNT. Ran Cotto's onboarding into the PUG Spring/April rotation: intake form + POs (3/2-3/3, 'No product should be sent without a PO'), marketing/newsletter signup (3/10, Kendall cleared to sign up for more than one sampling), team samples (3/18-3/19, Kendall allowed to hand-deliver rather than ship refrigerated), rotation kickoff (4/9). status: LIVE, REORDERING. Handled POs and delivery windows. 4/6: Kendall asked to raise the Cotto retail price to $12; Maggie: 'Updated, thanks!' — same-day approval. 4/7: confirmed delivery acceptance from 10AM. POs in window: MAI-5097 (3/20), MAI-5156 (4/10), MAI-5194 (4/14, confirmed by Kendall for Thursday delivery).
review_window: Spring rotation April 10 – July 6, 2026
notes: Strong sell-through signal: on MAI-5156 the flagship replied 'Yes let's take a partial delivery sooner so we dont run out!!' and then 'We ran out of backstock of the buffalo by the end of the night! I spent all afternoon restocking it 😅'. Retail price moved to $12 on 4/6 with no pushback.

status: FIRST RETAIL ACCOUNT, LIVE. Inbound 2/6: 'Kendall! We want Cotto! Let me know the best way to order.' Kendall confirmed 2/26 she'd be ready to ship the week of 3/9 and asked their category margin. Frances 2/27: 'We typically shoot for 60-65% margin in this category. Happy to meet you half way and increase the SRP so the margin doesn't come solely from a discount on your end.' Kendall countered 2/27 with MSRP $14.00, wholesale $5.60/unit / $33.60/case, 6 units/case, plus sell sheet. Launched ~3/9 and sold through in under 24 hours. Escalated into an INVESTMENT conversation: Frances 4/3 — 'supporting founders at this stage has been one of our favorite parts of building Meadow Lane. Sammy's gotten particularly excited...' — call held 4/8 4:30pm; Kendall's 4/9 recap: 'think the concept of a Meadow Lane venture fund is super interesting. I'll keep you in the loop.'
notes: Evy (evy@meadowlane.nyc) 'manages all of our inventory / purchasing / data and can help with ordering in the future'; Sammy (sammy@meadowlane.nyc) is the investment-side principal. Meadow Lane is Cotto's velocity proof point — Kendall cited '180 U/S/S/W just at Meadow Lane' to Misfits. Pricing here (MSRP $14 / $5.60 wholesale) is the anchor she later held with Butterfield.

status: LIVE consignment/rotation account, with an unresolved terms dispute. 2/3 COTTO ROTATION 2 contract sent; signatures complete 2/3. 2/13: The Shelf NYC extended through March 8. 3/16: 'we'd love to have you on The Shelf every week' — weekly contract; Kendall paid a $150 retainer 3/16 (invoice #000357-001). 3/17-3/19 Kendall gave a testimonial for The Shelf's deck. 4/13 Sloane chased an incomplete form; Kendall asked her to 'update to 18 units per sku per week? Or just an increment of 6 (since I am selling in cases now)'. Chased again 4/14: 'Just following up so I can complete ASAP - can you update weekly quantities?' STALLED — Sloane never updated the quantities. (Kendall's 4/27 note: 'was waiting for you to update the terms per above ask. Happy to pay as soon as you update the language, I just can't commit operationally to split cases so quantities...')
doors_or_stores: Moving to a permanent location at 58 W 8th Street (announced 4/14; subletting for a month while the lease is finalized)
review_window: Weekly rotation contracts; NYC run extended to March 8, then weekly
notes: ⚠ Open blocker: Cotto now sells in cases of 6 and cannot split cases; The Shelf's contract language still assumes unit-level quantities. Payment is being withheld pending the fix. Sloane Kraftsow, 305-469-4174.

status: Intro'd 4/6 by Nicole Krasnow Smith (Many Thanks Consulting). Sara same day: 'I've been hearing a lot about Cotto and would love to try and carry the line. Can you please send me samples?' Then pushed on price: 'we typically work off a 50% margin, so a cost of $6.60 would give us a retail of at least $12.99. Would it be possible to start at [that]...' Kendall HELD THE LINE 4/6: 'I'm not able to move on the wholesale price right now, but I'm happy to work with whatever retail price makes sense for you; I think $12.99 would be fine if...' No reply from Sara after 4/6 within the window.
notes: Sara asked for a $6.60 cost to hit 50% margin at $12.99 retail; Kendall refused to discount wholesale and offered retail-price flexibility instead. Samples were promised to her office. Sara's last name not stated in any email — uncertain.

We would love to try Cotto, here is a good address to send to: 414 East 75th Street 5th Floor, New York, NY...' Kendall 4/8: 'I'll plan to drop off samples early next week if that works for you.' Samantha 4/8: 'Sounds great, looking forward to trying!' Samples due to be hand-delivered the week of 4/13; no confirmation of drop-off in the window.

status: Intro'd 2/3 by Vincent (Step Two Advisors): 'I wanted to introduce you to John and Steve who are the cofounders of Nounos creamery and Meraki direct. Meraki direct is a DSD distributor in the tri-state area.' Call 'Cotto <> Meraki/Nounos' held 2/11 10:30am (both Steve and John Belesis accepted). Went quiet for two months. Kendall RE-ENGAGED 4/14: 'Wanted to circle back from our conversation in February. A lot has happened on our end since then and I think the timing might be right to revisit working together.' No reply within the window — Steve responded 4/20: 'Thats wonderful news, You have a great product so it's not a surprise. How is your production? Are you able to keep up?'
notes: John Belesis (john@nounoscreamery.com) is the other co-founder, cc'd throughout — Meraki/Nounos are linked. Schmidt's Country Market independently named Meraki as a distributor they already buy through, so Meraki is a two-sided fit. Steve's first question on re-engagement was production capacity, which is Cotto's known constraint.

I'm excited about this concept but approvals will be crucial to moving forward.' Kendall met Jen IN PERSON at Expo West and she sampled the product. _coverage: Full window 2026-02-01 through 2026-04-15 swept. Week-by-week: Feb 1-7, Feb 8-14, Feb 15-21, Feb 22-28, Mar 1-7 (Expo West, Mar 3-6), Mar 8-14, Mar 15-21 (Fairway Find win), Mar 22-28 (Fairway/Dora's/Rainforest/Target intros), Mar 29-Apr 4 (Misfits approval), Apr 5-11 (Target call, Butterfield/Agata intros), Apr 12-15. Because broad OR queries kept returning a capped resultCountEstimate of 201-402 dominated by newsletters, coverage was achieved by layering ~14 orthogonal query angles rather than paginating one query: (1) broad retail-keyword sweep per month with noise-domain exclusions; (2) high-signal term sweep ('sell sheet','line sheet','case pack','category review','new item','vendor setup','forager','category manager','buying group','wholesale price','opening order','purchase order','shelf reset','new vendor'); (3) named-distributor sweep (UNFI, KeHE, Rainforest, Meraki, Eco-Meal, Localee, Dora's, Bozzuto, C&S, AWG, INFRA, NCG, Pod Foods, Mable, Faire, Good Stuff, GFI, Hungryroot, Thrive, Good Eggs, Misfits); (4) named-retailer sweep (Fairway, Whole Foods, Sprouts, Fresh Market, Fresh Thyme, Wegmans, Target, Erewhon, Foxtrot, Pop Up Grocer, Happier, Meadow Lane, Westside, Zabar, Citarella, Gristedes, Morton Williams, DeCicco, Stew Leonard, Wakefern, ShopRite, Harmons, Central Market, Butterfield, Sag Harbor, Schmidt, plus Bristol Farms/Gelson/Publix/Kroger/Costco/BJ's/Sam's); (5) per-domain from:/to: sweeps for misfitsmarket.com, wholefoods.com, popupgrocer.com, happier.nyc, meadowlane.nyc, theshelfmiami.com, merakidirect.co, target.com, unfi.com; (6) in:sent sweeps by half-month; (7) Expo West follow-up batch (subject:'Following up'/'Great meeting'/'Expo', Mar 6-23); (8) inbound-inquiry language sweep ('carry Cotto','our store','grocery store','market','bodega','co-op','deli'); (9) entity sweep for Youngstown/HEPCO/Moscoe/Summit CPG/Dairy Delivery/Nounos/Step Two/Last Minute Market/Double L; (10) 'Up Next'/UpNext/Airgoods/Wakefern/Gourmet Garage sweep. Thread bodies read in full via get_thread for all ambiguous or decision-bearing threads (Fairway Find next steps, Misfits, UNFI UpNext both cycles, Hungryroot, Good Stuff, HEPCO, Youngstown, Instacart, Luke Beckstead/Harmons, Abby Lipman).
_notes: KEY FINDINGS. (1) The Fairway Find win (~3/18-19) is the hinge of the entire window: it produced 5 Wakefern contacts, both distributor intros (Rainforest, Dora's), press, and the credential Kendall used to re-open UNFI UpNext. (2) Rainforest is the most advanced distributor — supplier agreement in hand 3/27, redlines in progress 4/2, not signed by 4/15; two independent entry points (Perry Blatt→Jimmy Sebbah, and Expo West→Matthew Alongi). (3) Misfits Market is APPROVED for a 12-week innovation test, Deli & Prepared Foods, LA/LAX DC only, Ranch+Buffalo (French Onion explicitly excluded on assortment overlap), launching May; only open blocker is TraceGains access.
